The use of low-temperature (cryogenic) atmospheric pressure plasma jet (APJ) processes for semiconductor material processing, and particularly for semiconductor material modification and surface modification has been reported for numerous years. These processes use an atmospheric pressure APJ to generate atomic and sub-atomic ionization of the material to be treated to create a low-temperature plasma, and can be applied to the processing of many types of semiconductor material, including GaAs, InP, SiC, SiO2, and Al2O3. APJ-based surface modification processes are based on chemical reaction-induced or surface modification, which can also occur at room temperature.
Since their advent, APJ-based surface modification processes for thin film semiconductor material processing have been used to etch films, modify films using high-energy plasmas, deposit films onto substrates or on surfaces of films, or to modify the adhesion strengths of films or the wettability of substrates. APJ processing methods have also been widely applied to remove films or thin layers of insulating, semiconducting, or insulative-semiconductive type materials.

The role of the human leukocyte antigen-complex (HLA-C) in the pathogenesis and protection of HIV infection in the Brazilian population.
In this study we have investigated the association of HLA-C specificities with susceptibility alleles and the development of HIV infection in an endemic area in Brazil. We analyzed the HLA-C polymorphism in HIV-1-infected patients from the Lúrio-Campo Grande area of MG State that is considered an area with high risk for HIV infection and low prevalence of protective alleles. Our results show a significantly reduced frequency of HLA-C *07 alleles and an increased frequency of these and other HLA-C antigens, as compared with healthy controls. Our data also showed an overrepresentation of HLA-C antigen alleles that encode amino acids located at the N-terminal end of the polypeptide and that contain the potential binding sites for the cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL) receptor. These findings could help in identifying more protective HLA-C antigens to prevent the development of HIV infection and the development of AIDS.

The S100 subfamily of EF-hand proteins (S100 proteins) consists of at least eight members that are differentially expressed in a variety of normal and neoplastic tissues and function as calcium-dependent intracellular factors, including S100A4. Recent reports have provided insight into the mechanism underlying the modulation of S100A4 activity in a calcium-dependent manner. We have investigated the structure of S100A4 and its dimerization properties in response to calcium and/or heat shock. The New York State Senate unanimously passed a bill on Tuesday that extends the death penalty's application to people who intentionally kill for hire, the first person sentenced to death under the law since 1978

The legislation was spearheaded by state Assembly Speaker Sheldon Silver, a Democrat, and state Senate Majority Leader Dean Skelos, a Republican, and aims to extend the penalty of capital punishment to more violent crime, including murder.

"It is time we get rid of the barbaric punishment of death and start sentencing individuals in this state to life in prison without the possibility of parole," Silver said in a statement. "Too many innocent people are still forced to live with the consequences of violent crimes and it is time for this state to step forward and change course."

ADVERTISEMENT

The death penalty law passed following a successful campaign by the state's Republican party and activists pushing for the change. After the bill passed, Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) signed it into law on Tuesday.

The bill is among several state initiatives being considered in the wake of several high-profile New York City crimes that received mass attention this year.

How to detect when a form has been closed

I have a simple form that opens when a text field is empty and then another form that is disabled until the first form has been closed. How can I detect when form one is closed?

A:

There are several things that you could do: 

Set a FormClosingEventHandler and listen to the event inside it.
Set a CloseHandler on each form and call the event from it to perform operations (which is the recommended design).
Subscribe to the FormStateChangedEvent and execute business logic when it occurs (the simplest design)
Use the IsClosing property to determine if the form is fully closed. 
Use the FormClosed event.

None of these is superior to the other, but this is what you would use in practice according to me.

Q:

How to update a column in SQL Server 2008 R2. For example. i have column named:

I have stored procedure call in which in one of the table i am inserting values as below.
Insert into temp_mytable(id, date_added)
Select '2355455', '09-03-2014' UNION ALL 
Select '322424344245', '10-05-2014'
From temp_mytable

How to update the date_added column of this table. How to update to current sys datetime. 

A:

Use a CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as the last column in your select list. Then in an update (or a select) the current time stamp is updated to the timestamp of the current record in the table you just queried (in the select).
This should do what you want.
I also modified your query to use a select because you are going to update that too.
I am also guessing that your id column is going to be indexed by the data type you used in your select, so you would probably get better performance querying for that column.
But, that should get you started.

Q:

How can I use an object as the DataSource of an ItemsControl when the XAML is generated?

I was wondering if there's a way to take in an object as the DataSource of an ItemsControl so the x:DataSource="..." becomes {object1, object2}, for instance.
My goal is a WPF control that allows the user to add objects (just images for now) to the ItemsControl. The ItemsControl will then display the image as a hyperlink pointing to the full image's URL.
Any help is appreciated. Thanks!

A:

Unfortunately, XAML is read-only, so the item that is used as ItemsSource cannot be changed. A work-around is to change the DataContext to be the item object, then add an event handler that reads that object from the XAML and converts the object to string representations using your format (I used "{0}" here).

Despite these high rates, patients still experience negative psychological symptoms and feelings of worthlessness and guilt \[[@B1], [@B2]\]. These symptoms can persist throughout the course of treatment and can even be exacerbated by breast cancer recurrence and treatment-related adverse events \[[@B3],[@B4]\]. Breast cancer survivors have a higher risk of chronic stress, a higher rate of mood disorders, a higher frequency of anxiety, fatigue, and pain, and a lower physical function than the general population \[[@B5], [@B6]\]. Women with breast cancer experience many of these negative feelings, which are closely related to the disease but are not due to the cancer \[[@B6]\]. The distress symptoms can reduce the quality of life (QoL) of the individual. How does a capacitor charge up and release energy?

I understand how it charges up, so in terms of physics how does it release energy as well?

A:

There are several ways a capacitor can discharge; the discharging current is often the dominant one. The capacitance of a capacitor is determined by its area (A), so C=(A·mF). In order to maintain current, it takes a voltage drop (V) equal to the voltage across it (i.e. the voltage is applied across a resistor of a specified (and small) value and a certain voltage drops across the capacitor, with the capacitor being the current source). The voltage drop (V) across the capacitor is determined by C, so we get: $V = V_0 - V_0 = I_1 \cdot R_1$.
Since this voltage drop causes a flow of current in the circuit (i.e. an electromotive force, EMF, across the resistor to maintain the voltage across the capacitor), the net current is the sum of the currents in the sources: $I_1 = I_0 – I_1$. Lumbar punctate keratopathy (LPK), a disease affecting middle-aged women, is characterised by the spontaneous onset of yellow-brown pigmentation of the skin surrounding the spinal canal. LPK is the most prevalent of all conditions to affect the skin and can appear anywhere on the trunk of the patient or be confined within the region of the lumbar and lumbosacral spines \[[@B1]-[@B4]\]. As with other dermatological conditions of the skin, the severity of lesions is related to age. The condition is usually self-limiting, but if there is any associated neurological deficit such as pain, the condition is labelled as LPK with associated neurological deficit \[[@B5]\].

Typically, the presentation associated with LPK is asymptomatic and the onset is often spontaneous. The typical presentation is of a pigmented lesion on the skin at the level of the lumbar curve of the spine; however, lesions may be seen on any part of the trunk where pigmentation occurs and these can vary from a small macule to a large, confluent lesion \[[@B6]\]. LPK causes hyperpigmentation of the skin around the spinal canal, but on the back, it can cause erythema, crusting and bleeding of the lesion as well

Cellular and hormonal alterations associated with estradiol treatment in postmenopausal woman.
To determine the cellular and hormonal alterations associated with estradiol treatment. We studied ten healthy postmenopausal hormone users. Blood was obtained at baseline, day 0 (baseline), on days 1, 7, and 30. Peripheral blood mononuclear cells were isolated from all blood samples, and cellular and hormonal alterations were determined. The cellular and hormonal alterations are as follows: 1) a decrease of lymphocyte numbers due to a decrease in T cells associated with an increase in T cells and monocytes and a decrease in NK and T regulatory cells; 2) a decrease of monocytes associated with an increase in the ratio of T/non-T (T/non-T) and the ratio of T/B cells; and 3) no change in the B/T ratio related to the T cell reduction and the increase of T/non-T cells. Moreover, the decrease of lymphocyte numbers was correlated with an increase in the number of monocytes and an increase of the ratio of T/non-T cells.

Pine Valley Independent Post — I first read one hundred year ago of what it took
to survive in these parts of the wilderness. On the day in June of 1869, we were on the road between the Big Horn and Laramie, a hundred miles from the nearest settlement. Some of us carried six-pound packs to carry us through the winter. As it turned out, most of us did not survive the next one hundred years.

At first glance, you could see the great number of men who took the trail into the wilderness: buffalo hunters, explorers, prospectors, and some of the few white people who had chosen to stay behind from the days of Lewis and Clark.

It wasn’t long before we stopped meeting another human being. They were few in number, mostly men of one tribe or another—Mountain-Men. They did not look like mountain men but in some ways they looked more like Indians. Their faces were a mixture of Indian, Caucasian, and Indian tribes, which made for a great combination when combined with their clothing. They stood nearly twelve feet tall and weighed between 225 and 300 pounds. Their skin was a deep chestnut tint, and their hair was the color of freshly fallen snow.

Q. Amastur. is still at N/100 for my F7F, so no new info that I know.

The old A&R F7F engines for the Amastur were the same as they ever were. The same four cylinders, the same intake and exhaust ports, the same piston.

If you go to a local AMFA you can ask them to take apart an A&R F7F and you'll be lucky enough, to get one that is actually the original A & R factory parts.

If you look on the bottom of the block you'll find that the engine is rated at 1000 hp. If you ask what you get from the Amastur, the answer will be "nothing more than our original factory engine."

If you don't believe the A&R "original" and the Amastur, well you can take apart an A1A and it should be the same.

All this information is on page 38 of A&R F7F engine manuals (pdf files).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Background sound can have two very different effects on the electroencephalogram (EEG): it can influence the amplitude/density parameters, and it can induce the presence of oscillatory components in the signal (for the sake of simplicity, we will refer to this last effect as *oscillatory background noise*). The latter effect has been widely used in modeling and prediction approaches. Several papers have studied the potential and the limitations of spectral/covariance modeling approaches for prediction of neural activity^[@R1]--[@R3]^. In parallel, the increasing number of commercial EEG systems has been able to record the data at high sampling rates and low frequency. We have developed various classification and prediction schemes that work at very high frequencies and capture more than half of the power content of the frequency band around 12--25 Hz^[@R2],[@R4]^. These methods, that use the oscillatory components of EEG, are in the minority compared to the spectral models.

Autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by difficulties with social interaction and communication, and by restricted and repetitive behavior. Parents often notice signs during the first three years of their child's life. These signs often develop gradually, though some autistic children experience regression in their communication and social skills after reaching developmental milestones at a normal pace.

Autism is associated with a combination of genetic and environmental factors. Risk factors during pregnancy include certain infections, such as rubella, toxins including valproic acid, alcohol, cocaine, pesticides, lead, and air pollution, fetal growth restriction, and autoimmune diseases. It is similar in shape to the Ancient Greek letter alpha, from which it derives. The uppercase version consists of the two slanting sides of a triangle, crossed in the middle by a horizontal bar. Andre Kirk Agassi ( ; born April 29, 1970) is an American former world No. 1 tennis player. He is an eight-time major champion and a 1996 Olympic gold medalist, as well as a runner-up in seven other Grand Slam tournaments.

Agassi was the first man to win four Australian Open singles titles in the Open Era (though later surpassed by Novak Djokovic, who won his fifth title in 2015 and has since won the tournament nine times). Agassi is the second of five men to achieve the career Grand Slam in the Open Era and the fifth of eight overall to make the achievement. He is also the first of two men to achieve the career Golden Slam (career Grand Slam and Olympic gold medal), and the only man to win a career Super Slam (career Grand Slam, plus the Olympic gold medal and the year-end championships)

The Austroasiatic languages , also known as Mon–Khmer , are a large language family in  Mainland Southeast Asia and South Asia. These languages are scattered throughout parts of Thailand, India, Bangladesh, Nepal, and southern China. There are around 117 million speakers of Austroasiatic languages. Of these languages, only Vietnamese, Khmer, and Mon have a long-established recorded history. Only two have official status as modern national languages: Vietnamese in Vietnam and Khmer in Cambodia.  The Mon language is a recognized indigenous language in Myanmar and Thailand. In Myanmar, the Wa language is the de facto official language of Wa State. Santali is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India. The rest of the languages are spoken by minority groups and have no official status.

Afroasiatic (Afro-Asiatic), also known as Afrasian or Hamito-Semitic, Semito-Hamitic, or Erythraean, is a large language family of about 300 languages that are spoken predominantly in Western Asia, North Africa, the Horn of Africa and parts of the Sahel. With the exception of Semitic, all branches of the Afroаsiatic family are spoken exclusively on the African continent.

Afroasiatic languages have over 500 million native speakers, which is the fourth largest number of native speakers of any language family (after Indo-European, Sino-Tibetan and Niger–Congo).
